step.title: Generate Health Check goal (single goal, phase=health_check)
step.rule: This step is UNCONDITIONAL — every project needs a health check endpoint.
depends_on MUST include EVERY infrastructure goal ID — dynamically collected from goals.yaml, not hardcoded (G-03).
Single goal — never split per-BC. Health check is a cross-cutting concern covering all services.

substep.title: Collect all infrastructure goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter all goal entries where phase=infrastructure. Collect their goal IDs into INFRA_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: ABORT with error: "No infrastructure goals found in goals.yaml — cannot generate health check goal without infrastructure dependencies"

substep.rule: This MUST be dynamic — BC count varies per project. Never hardcode goal IDs.

substep.title: Identify services from discovery outputs
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md and docs/architecture/bounded-contexts.md. Extract infrastructure services present in the project (database, Redis, message queue if selected).
Build SERVICES list containing only the services actually configured for this project.
substep.rule: Never hardcode the service list — derive from discovery outputs. Different projects may have only DB, or DB+Redis, or DB+Redis+queue.

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (HC-01..HC-03)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 3 health check criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:

criteria.item: GET /health returns 200 when all services up
GET /health returns 503 when a service is down
GET /health response includes env field equal to APP_ENV value and database field matching connected DB name (E2E-ENV-CONV)

substep.rule: All 3 criteria (plus HC-04 when HAS_DATABASE) must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with commands that verify the health check endpoint:

commands.cmd: curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" {{BASE_URL}}/health (expect 200; {{BASE_URL}} = test-environment.md Base URL / published host port, per HTTP-CONV — never a hardcoded :8000)
curl -s {{BASE_URL}}/health | jq . (expect JSON with per-service status keys matching SERVICES)

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Health check goal creates a single GET /health endpoint that reports system status with per-service checks for {{SERVICES}}. Depends on ALL infrastructure goals: {{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}} — these provide the services that health check monitors.

section.name: fan-out-hint
section.hint: single-task — one endpoint, one controller, one set of service checks.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Health endpoint response
investigator.pass: HTTP 200, JSON body contains status keys for each service in SERVICES, all marked healthy
investigator.fail: Non-200 status code, missing service keys, or non-JSON response

investigator.name: Degraded health check
investigator.commands: Integration test with mocked failing service — stop one service dependency, hit /health
investigator.pass: HTTP 503, response body shows failed service with unhealthy status while others remain healthy
investigator.fail: Still returns 200 when a service is down, or missing per-service status detail

section.rule: Exactly 2 investigators (GM-08 allows 2-4). Each has type, commands, and pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

section.name: not-in-scope
section.value: Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes, metrics endpoints, detailed performance diagnostics — these are deployment-specific concerns beyond the health check endpoint itself.

substep.title: Inject code rules (apply [CODE-RULE-INJECTION])
substep.action: Bind RULE_FILES = the comma-joined health-check deliverable paths (the GET /health controller + per-service check classes + their tests). Bind RULE_PHASE = `health_check` (this goal's phase token — pass it VERBATIM; do NOT remap it).

Run `tmux-cli rules match --files <RULE_FILES> --phase <RULE_PHASE> --json` and apply the spine's `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` procedure (<conventions> / <rule-to-goal-injection>): copy each NON-EMPTY `acceptance_line` into the ACCEPTANCE array (3.25.3); copy each `automated`/`mixed` `validate_cmd` into the VALIDATE array (3.25.4); fold `review` `validate_cmd`s into ONE convention-audit investigator (ADD; base is 2 from 3.25.5, so a slot is free — no drop); add the `Code rules: <ids> — read <paths>` line to CONTEXT (3.25.5). Empty `rules` ⇒ inject nothing.
substep.rule: The phase token `health_check` is NOT in the Go `allowedPhases` enum — `rules match` returns an empty set, so this goal UNDER-injects (fail-safe: under-enforce, never wrong-enforce — mechanical parity, exactly as 3.21.7b documents for `error_handling`). Do NOT remap the phase token to a real phase to force a match. PRESERVE the 2 existing investigators.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Create GET /health endpoint returning system status with per-service checks
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.25.3 (exactly 3 items, HC-01..HC-03)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.25.5 (purpose + fan-out hint)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.25.5's 2 investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
INFRA_GOAL_IDS — ALL infrastructure goal IDs collected in substep 3.25.1

substep.rule: Description is 73 characters — within the 120-char limit (MaxGoalDescriptionLength).
max_retries=3 (G-11) — health check is a single focused goal, fixable in few cycles.
depends_on MUST be the FULL INFRA_GOAL_IDS list — never just the last one (G-03).

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT.
Print: "Health check created: goal ID (phase=health_check, depends_on={{INFRA_GOAL_IDS}}) with 2 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3"
